videos.title: STANDOUTS & PARTY FLOATS! Carolina Hurricanes Standouts in the Stanley Cup Final & Parade Updates
videos.summary_source_text:
STANDOUTS & PARTY FLOATS! Carolina Hurricanes Standouts in the Stanley Cup Final & Parade Updates As we wrap up the final show of the Stanley Cup Championship Week for the Carolina Hurricanes, who were the biggest standouts in the final series in the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs? Who made the biggest impact en route to the Hurricanes' second Stanley Cup? Also, what should fans expect on Saturday for the Championship Parade in downtown Raleigh? On the Friday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on who a couple of the biggest standouts were in the Stanley Cup Final against the Vegas Golden Knights. Who stood out in terms of making an impact as the Canes bring home another Stanley Cup title? Could it be the Conn Smythe winner? Possible goalies? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on who impressed him the most in terms os going above and beyond for the Hurricanes. Who made a lasting impression throughout the playoffs and the Cup Final? Finally, Zach concludes the show giving updates on the Championship Parade, which will be on Saturday in downtown Raleigh. When does it start? Where is free parking? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: GLM 5.2 Failed... But Not At Everything
videos.summary_source_text:
GLM 5.2 Failed... But Not At Everything 🎖️ Get Tank and join the community: https://mrc.fm/cmc

A Chinese lab dropped GLM 5.2. Open weights, free, and it just beat Fable 5 on design. So I ran it in Claude Code via Ollama and made it build three games. Then I pitched it against Claude Opus 4.8 inside my Tank framework. Same prompts. Same machine. One winner. Did the free open model live up to the hype? Watch and decide.

videos.title: As NHL Offseason Activity Picks Up, All Quiet On The Dallas Stars Front | DLLS Stars Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
As NHL Offseason Activity Picks Up, All Quiet On The Dallas Stars Front | DLLS Stars Podcast Become a DIEHARD! alldlls.com/diehard

The Stanley Cup has been awarded and the 2026 NHL Entry Draft is just over one week away. Several offseason trades have happened in the last few days, Vegas and Toronto announced new head coaches, Utah and Washington announced contract extensions for their head coaches and the Golden Knights (sort of) allowed a team to speak to Bruce Cassidy. And the NHL just wrapped up its investigation into Mike Babcock, clearing him to return to coaching if a team wants to hire him (cough…Edmonton…cough). However, it’s been fully quiet on the Dallas Stars front, with the only recent news, which was quite big, involved the team’s future arena plans moving out of downtown Dallas and north to Plano. On today’s show, we will talk through the Holding Pattern of the Stars offseason moves and all the things going on around the NHL. And perhaps, maybe, today is the day we finally discuss the NHL’s new ALL-Star game format - which has been bumped from at least three shows in the last week.

18.06.26 - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

videos.title: The All-Goalie Episode w/ Kevin Woodley | Daily Faceoff Live
videos.summary_source_text:
The All-Goalie Episode w/ Kevin Woodley | Daily Faceoff Live It's an all-goalie edition of Daily Faceoff Live.

Tyler Yaremchuk and Carter Hutton break down the NHL's evolving goalie market, looking at the biggest names who could be available through free agency and trade this offseason.

The guys discuss Sergei Bobrovsky's future, the best goaltenders available on the open market, and some under-the-radar names who could end up becoming valuable additions for teams looking to upgrade between the pipes.

On the trade front, which teams should be calling the Vegas Golden Knights about Adin Hill? Should the Buffalo Sabres move one of their four NHL-caliber goaltenders? Could Jordan Binnington make sense somewhere else? And if Connor Hellebuyck were to become available, where would the reigning Vezina winner fit best?

They also take a closer look at the Edmonton Oilers' goaltending situation. Who should Edmonton target to play alongside Tristan Jarry next season? Is a young goalie like Sebastian Cossa worth pursuing? Would a veteran such as Frederik Andersen make more sense? Or should they consider bringing back Connor Ingram?

Kevin Woodley of InGoal Magazine joins the show to discuss what teams can learn from the Carolina Hurricanes' Stanley Cup-winning goaltending strategy, whether more NHL clubs should carry three goaltenders, and which goalie reclamation projects could pay off next season.

videos.title: Dak Prescott: George Pickens Makes EVERYTHING Easier for the Cowboys Offense
videos.summary_source_text:
Dak Prescott: George Pickens Makes EVERYTHING Easier for the Cowboys Offense Dallas Cowboys quarterback Dak Prescott met with reporters at OTAs and discussed the impact of new wide receiver George Pickens on the offense.

Prescott explained why Pickens makes life easier for the Cowboys offense, what the talented receiver brings to Dallas, and why he wasn't surprised to see Pickens participate in OTAs despite signing his franchise tag. Dak also shared his thoughts on the chemistry-building process and expectations for the Cowboys heading into the 2026 season.

Watch the full Dak Prescott media availability from Cowboys OTAs and hear everything he had to say about George Pickens, the offense, and the upcoming season.

#DallasCowboys #DakPrescott #GeorgePickens #CowboysNation #NFL #CowboysOTAs #DallasCowboysNews Dallas Cowboys quarterback Dak Prescott met with reporters at OTAs and discussed the impact of new wide receiver George Pickens on the offense.

Prescott explained why Pickens makes life easier for the Cowboys offense, what the talented receiver brings to Dallas, and why he wasn't surprised to see Pickens participate in OTAs despite signing his franchise tag. Dak also shared his thoughts on the chemistry-building process and expectations for the Cowboys heading into the 2026 season.

Watch the full Dak Prescott media availability from Cowboys OTAs and hear everything he had to say about George Pickens, the offense, and the upcoming season.

#DallasCowboys #DakPrescott #GeorgePickens #CowboysNation #NFL #CowboysOTAs #DallasCowboysNews

videos.title: Could Moving Val Nichushkin Be the Next Move For the Colorado Avalanche?
videos.summary_source_text:
Could Moving Val Nichushkin Be the Next Move For the Colorado Avalanche? Colorado Avalanche face a critical crossroads—will Valeri Nichushkin be the next big name on the move as the roster shuffle continues? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down the potential fallout from the Ross Colton trade, discuss Nichushkin’s inconsistency on and off the ice, and question if a fresh start could benefit both player and team. Is Colorado ready to part ways with a once-vital piece, or will Nichushkin rebound as a core contributor?

The conversation also unpacks a heated NHL offseason, from players requesting trades to Kelly McCrimmon’s headline-grabbing comments about the Vegas Golden Knights’ reputation. The hosts explore how Avalanche culture keeps stars like Nathan MacKinnon and Cale Makar content and loyal, what it would take to shake that bond, and why Vegas draws so much ire from around the league. Can Colorado’s front office make the right moves to stay atop the Stanley Cup hunt?

Ron Chenoy-Imagn Images

videos.title: Gemini CLI Dies Today... Meet Antigravity CLI
videos.summary_source_text:
Gemini CLI Dies Today... Meet Antigravity CLI Join the community. Get Tank, meet other AI builders 👉 https://mrc.fm/cmc

I tested the new agy command to see what survived. Antigravity CLI is built in Go, runs async multi-agent workflows, and keeps Agent Skills, Hooks, Subagents and plugins. There's no 1:1 feature parity and it's closed source now. Enterprise and paid API users keep Gemini CLI for now. I'll show you how to migrate before your scripts, cron jobs and CI break.

videos.title: Microsoft And DeepSeek Are Launching New AI Together
videos.summary_source_text:
Microsoft And DeepSeek Are Launching New AI Together Microsoft and DeepSeek may be coming together inside Copilot Cowork, and the timing is huge. Microsoft just made Cowork generally available worldwide with usage-based billing, while Axios reports that DeepSeek could become a lower-cost model option hosted on Azure. The bigger story is that Microsoft is turning Copilot into a multi-model AI agent platform built for real company work.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com

📌 What You’ll See:
Microsoft Copilot Cowork is now generally available
SOURCE: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/blog/2026/06/16/copilot-cowork-is-now-generally-available/
Microsoft weighs DeepSeek for Copilot Cowork
SOURCE: https://www.axios.com/2026/06/16/microsoft-copilot-cowork-tokenmaxxing-cowork

videos.title: The Footwork To Catch More Passes
videos.summary_source_text:
The Footwork To Catch More Passes The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: The biggest winners and losers from the Denver Broncos’ offseason
videos.summary_source_text:
The biggest winners and losers from the Denver Broncos’ offseason Bo Nix took another encouraging step in his recovery as the Denver Broncos completed their final practice of the offseason before training camp.
The DNVR Broncos Crew breaks down everything that happened at Denver Broncos mandatory minicamp, including Bo Nix’s workload, the biggest offensive and defensive standouts and what Sean Payton’s team showed during competitive team periods. How close is Nix to being fully cleared? Which players helped themselves before the summer break? And who has the early advantage in the Broncos’ wide receiver competition?
The guys also examine Denver’s battle for the WR3 job, the growing excitement surrounding Jaylen Waddle and how the explosive receiver could transform Sean Payton and Davis Webb’s offense. Plus, which Broncos position battles and roster storylines will matter most when training camp begins?
The DNVR Broncos Podcast also discusses the best moves from around the NFL and what they could mean for Denver’s pursuit of an AFC championship during the 2026 NFL season.

On the DNVR Broncos Podcast, Ryan Koenigsberg and Henry Chisholm are coming to you LIVE from the DNVR Bar to break down all of the latest news in Broncos Country.

videos.summary_source_text:
2026 NHL Draft Recap - The Sheet Special Edition with Jeff Marek and Pierre McGuire The first round is complete, the picks are in, and now it's time to break down everything that happened on one of the biggest nights of the NHL offseason.

Join Jeff Marek and Pierre McGuire immediately following the conclusion of the first round of the 2026 NHL Draft for a special live Draft Recap Show. From surprise selections and shocking slides to blockbuster trades and bold moves by NHL general managers, Jeff and Pierre analyze every major storyline that unfolded throughout the night. Which teams nailed their picks? Which organizations took the biggest risks? And which prospects found themselves in the perfect situation to begin their NHL journey?

Jeff and Pierre break down every first-round selection, evaluate the winners and losers from draft night, discuss the biggest trades and rumors that emerged, and examine how the landscape of the NHL may have shifted heading into free agency and the rest of the offseason. They'll also react to unexpected moments, controversial decisions, and the prospects who generated the most buzz around the hockey world.

Whether your favorite team made a franchise-changing selection or you're looking for expert analysis on every move from the opening round, this is your complete breakdown of the 2026 NHL Draft's first night. Don't miss Jeff Marek and Pierre McGuire as they react live to all the drama, excitement, and surprises from Round 1.

videos.title: 2026 NHL Draft Preview - The Sheet Special Edition with Jeff Marek and Pierre McGuire
videos.summary_source_text:
2026 NHL Draft Preview - The Sheet Special Edition with Jeff Marek and Pierre McGuire The future of the NHL begins here.

Join hockey insiders Jeff Marek and Pierre McGuire for a special live 2026 NHL Draft Preview Show, streaming one hour before the first pick is made on June 26, 2026. As NHL front offices make their final preparations, Jeff and Pierre break down everything you need to know heading into one of the most unpredictable nights on the hockey calendar. From the race for the No. 1 overall selection to the biggest risers and fallers on draft boards, they'll analyze where the top prospects could land, which teams are looking to make a splash, and what surprises could shake up the first round.

Will we see blockbuster trades? Could teams move up to target their future franchise cornerstone? Which prospects are generating the most buzz behind the scenes? Jeff and Pierre examine the latest NHL Draft rumors, discuss organizational needs across the league, identify potential steals, and offer their final predictions before the draft officially gets underway. Whether you're a diehard fan tracking every prospect or simply looking to get caught up before the picks start flying, this is your ultimate guide to the 2026 NHL Draft.

Don't miss this special pre-draft edition as Jeff Marek and Pierre McGuire set the stage for an unforgettable night that could reshape the future of the NHL.

videos.title: Hurricanes weather the Cup Final storm | Quest for the Stanley Cup Ep. 4
videos.summary_source_text:
Hurricanes weather the Cup Final storm | Quest for the Stanley Cup Ep. 4 In Episode 4 of Quest For The Stanley Cup, the behind-the-scenes documentary series chronicling the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs, we follow the tense six-game battle between the Carolina Hurricanes and Golden Knights in the Stanley Cup Final. Exclusive, one-of-a-kind mic'd up audio from every game captures each player's reactions in real time, as well as the motivational tactics used by head coaches Rod Brind'Amour and John Tortorella to fire their boys up. Hear from players such as captain and Conn Smythe Trophy winner Jordan Staal, Logan Stankoven, and Brandon Bussi, as they share candid insights during the series. Learn how the Hurricanes' innovative "Blue Collar Presses" in the gym directly prepared them for a memorable Stanley Cup celebration.

Quest for the Stanley Cup airs on the NHL’s YouTube channel at the beginning of each round, taking fans on a whirlwind tour of the playoffs as teams vie for the most coveted trophy in professional sports – the Stanley Cup. 

▶️ SUBSCRIBE: https://youtube.com/nhl

videos.title: The Pushup That Beats Regular Pushups for Chest Growth
videos.summary_source_text:
The Pushup That Beats Regular Pushups for Chest Growth Want to build a bigger chest with just push ups? The problem might not be that push ups don’t work. It might be that you’re doing the same push up variation over and over again and expecting your chest to grow from it.

Build Muscle Without Weights - https://athleanx.com/xero
Subscribe to This Channel Here: http://bit.ly/2b0coMW

In this video, Jeff Cavaliere breaks down one of the most overlooked push up techniques for chest growth: the prisoner push up. At first glance, it may look like a half-rep push up, but that is exactly the point. By staying in the lower portion of the push up, you can shift more of the focus onto the chest and shoulders while reducing how much the triceps limit the set.

A traditional full range of motion push up is still one of the best bodyweight exercises for building the upper body. It trains the chest, shoulders, triceps and core all at once. But when your goal is to build a bigger chest with bodyweight training, the top portion of the push up can become a problem. As you press toward lockout, the triceps take over more of the work. That means your triceps may fatigue before your chest gets enough volume to grow.

That is where the prisoner push up becomes so effective.

Instead of locking out every rep, you stay in the bottom range of motion, where the chest has to do more of the work. This allows you to accumulate more chest-focused volume, create more metabolic stress in the pecs and continue the set without the triceps becoming the limiting factor too early. If you have ever felt push ups more in your arms than your chest, this is a variation you need to try.

When training with bodyweight exercises, the mechanical tension is usually lower than what you would get from heavy dumbbell bench presses or barbell bench presses. That means volume and intensity become even more important. If you want push ups to build muscle, you need to find ways to increase the challenge, increase the number of quality reps, shorten rest periods and make each variation more targeted.

In this video, Jeff shows how to use the prisoner push up as a chest-building tool, then how to progress it with harder push up variations that keep the same bottom-range focus. These include angled push ups, staggered push ups, more explosive push ups and archer push ups. Each variation gives you a different way to overload the chest without needing a bench, dumbbells, cables or a full gym setup.

The key is understanding what actually builds muscle: mechanical tension, eccentric overload and metabolic stress. With push ups, you may not get the same heavy loading you would from weights, but you can still create an effective chest workout by manipulating range of motion, tempo, exercise difficulty, rep volume and rest periods.

If your chest workouts at home have stopped working, or if regular push ups feel too easy, this video will show you how to make push ups harder and more effective for building muscle. You don’t need to abandon push ups. You just need to know how to use them the right way.

Try adding prisoner push ups to your next chest workout and see how quickly your pecs start to light up. Focus on staying low, keeping tension on the chest, pushing through the heel of your hands and driving every rep with intent. As you get stronger, move to more advanced push up variations while still keeping the chest under constant tension.

If you are looking for a complete step-by-step bodyweight workout program that shows you how to build muscle without weights, check out ATHLEAN-X XERO at https://athleanx.com/xero. It is designed to help you build a lean, muscular body using bodyweight training, smart exercise progressions and the same science-based approach used in every ATHLEAN-X program.

Whether your goal is to build a bigger chest, get better at push ups, train at home, build muscle without equipment or finally make bodyweight chest workouts feel challenging again, this video will show you how to get more out of every rep.

For more science-based workouts, bodyweight exercises, chest exercises, push up variations and muscle building tips, make sure to subscribe to ATHLEAN-X and turn on notifications so you never miss a new video. videos.title: JUST A PINCH IN YOUR WATER CAN CHANGE HOW YOU FEEL ALL DAY | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
JUST A PINCH IN YOUR WATER CAN CHANGE HOW YOU FEEL ALL DAY | Dr. Mandell What if one of the simplest things you could do each morning was also one of the most overlooked? In this video, Dr. Mandell explains why adding a tiny pinch of mineral-rich salt to a glass of water may help support your body's natural hydration process, electrolyte balance, nerve function, muscle function, circulation, and overall well-being throughout the day. Learn the science behind how sodium works together with water inside and outside your cells, why hydration is about much more than just drinking water, and how a simple morning habit may help support energy, mental clarity, physical performance, and healthy body function. You'll also learn the proper amount to use, the best types of salt, and who may benefit most from this easy daily practice. videos.title: NHL Clears Mike Babcock, Vegas Hires Ryan Craig, and Toronto News ft. Emily Kaplan & Greg Wyshynski
videos.summary_source_text:
NHL Clears Mike Babcock, Vegas Hires Ryan Craig, and Toronto News ft. Emily Kaplan & Greg Wyshynski Jeff Marek and Greg Wyshynski are back with a fresh edition of The Sheet as they tackle some of the biggest stories shaping the NHL offseason. ESPN's Emily Kaplan joins the show to break down the latest developments surrounding the Mike Babcock situation and what it could mean for the league moving forward. Jeff and Greg also dive into the ongoing Toronto Maple Leafs coaching search, examining where things stand after the hiring of Jim Hiller and what other changes could still be on the horizon in Toronto. Plus, they discuss the growing speculation surrounding Matthew Knies and whether the young star winger could realistically become trade bait this summer as the Leafs continue to reshape their roster. The conversation also shifts to Las Vegas, where the Vegas Golden Knights have moved on from John Tortorella and turned to Ryan Craig as their next head coach, sparking a discussion about the organization's direction following another disappointing playoff exit. All that and more on another jam-packed offseason edition of The Sheet with Jeff Marek and Greg Wyshynski.

videos.title: This Optical illusion Is Secretly Ruining Your Golf Swing
videos.summary_source_text:
This Optical illusion Is Secretly Ruining Your Golf Swing This could be the reason you never solve your driver problems.What if I told you that an optical illusion is causing your 2 way miss and you do not even know it?

Most golfers spend years changing their swing, grip and technique trying to fix shots that go left one hole and right the next. The truth is the problem may have nothing to do with your swing at all.

In this video, Alex Elliott reveals the hidden visual mistake that is secretly ruining your driver and shows you the simple adjustment that can transform your accuracy almost instantly.

You will discover how the optical illusion is tricking you every single round, why your driver miss keeps switching between left and right, how to set up correctly for straighter more consistent drives, the simple drill Alex uses to help golfers hit more fairways, and how to gain confidence every time you stand on the tee.

If you are tired of losing balls, missing fairways and never knowing where your next drive is going, this is a video you cannot afford to skip.

videos.summary_source_text:
Tyler Toffoli answers your questions on Never Offside | S2, Ep 29 Julie and Cat are joined by Cat’s husband and San Jose Sharks forward Tyler Toffoli to recap Cat’s recent surprise birthday party in New York City. Tyler and Julie share the backstory of their devious plan, including a fake sponsor, bogus staged calls with the show’s production crew, and deliberately planted text messages, all designed to lure Cat to NYC without any hesitation only to replace trickery with a celebration. The show concludes with Tyler answering fan questions, including which teammate he’d most trust with his cell phone and his favorite goal of his career.

videos.title: Why Do Some People Recover Better Than Others?
videos.summary_source_text:
Why Do Some People Recover Better Than Others? Some patients seem to tolerate major surgery remarkably well, while others struggle with complications during recovery. Looking deeper suggests that the difference wasn't just the procedure itself. It was how well the body could balance its stress response. That pattern suggests that health isn't just about managing stress, it's about maintaining balance in the face of it.

videos.title: Lucas Wallmark – lämnade mångmiljonbelopp på bordet
videos.summary_source_text:
Lucas Wallmark – lämnade mångmiljonbelopp på bordet Shoot The Puck är en podcast av hockeynews.se

Lucas Wallmark är på väg att få göra sin a-lagsdebut i moderklubben Björklöven och det händer samtidigt som det är 25 år sedan klubben spelade i SHL.
30-åringen fortsätter att gå sin egen väg.
Han lämnade NHL som 25-åring.
Han lämnar nu Schweiz vid 30 års ålder – och många miljoner på bordet för att uppleva drömmen i hemstaden.
Hockeynews Henrik Sjöberg har träffat Wallmark i Umeå och gått till botten med beslutet.

Och mycket annat:

videos.title: Your Irons Have A Secret Superpower… and You're Ignoring It
videos.summary_source_text:
Your Irons Have A Secret Superpower… and You're Ignoring It Most golfers are trying to do something with their irons that actually makes ball striking harder.

The truth is, your iron club already has a built-in superpower — it is designed to get the ball airborne. Yet many golfers instinctively try to help the ball into the air by leaning back, flipping their wrists, and adding loft through impact. Unfortunately, that is exactly what leads to poor strikes, weak shots, and inconsistent distance.

In this video, I use a simple demonstration with a ping pong bat to show why this instinct makes sense, why it works with a flat surface, and why it causes so many problems with a golf club.

Once you understand what your irons are designed to do, you can stop fighting the club and start creating cleaner contact, better compression, and more consistent iron shots.

If you struggle with thin shots, fat shots, weak strikes, or inconsistent ball striking, this is a concept you need to understand.

WASN'T EXPECTING THAT! 😱 McLaren Series 3 Irons #mclaren #series3 #golf #golfgear #subscribe 

Are the McLaren Series 3 Irons one of the best-feeling irons on the market right now?

In this Series 3 Iron Review, I put these irons through a full on-course and launch monitor test to see whether the performance matches the hype. What immediately stood out was the incredible feel at impact, but what impressed me even more was the consistency on mishits across the entire iron set.

Many irons perform well from the middle of the face, but the real test is how they respond when you don't strike them perfectly. The Series 3 irons delivered impressive distance control, forgiveness, and consistency even when contact wasn't ideal.

videos.title: Tesla Pi Phone 2026: The Truth Nobody Wants to Show You
videos.summary_source_text:
Tesla Pi Phone 2026: The Truth Nobody Wants to Show You Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
The Tesla Pi Phone is the most-hyped product with Elon Musk's name on it — and it's the one product that has never existed. No FCC filing. No prototype. No patent. Just a 2021 concept render the internet refused to let die.

So in this breakdown we do the BitBiased thing: we check the filing instead of the thumbnail. First, the real Musk machine of 2026 — the SpaceX–xAI merger, the Austin robotaxi that half-works, Starlink quietly printing money, Grok welded to the loudest feed on earth, and Optimus still stuck in prototype. 
Then the full Pi Phone reality check: where the myth came from, why it keeps rising from the dead, and the ten-second test for telling signal from hype.
If the only evidence is a render and a countdown timer, you already have your answer.

videos.title: The Trillion-Dollar Trick: How Musk Turned SpaceX Into an AI Empire
videos.summary_source_text:
The Trillion-Dollar Trick: How Musk Turned SpaceX Into an AI Empire Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everyone's reporting the number. Almost nobody's explaining what it actually means. When SpaceX rang the Nasdaq bell on June 12, 2026 under the ticker SPCX, Elon Musk's net worth crossed a trillion dollars — but this milestone has far less to do with one man's wealth than with who gets to fund the future of AI.

In this breakdown we cut through the headlines and walk the mechanics: why net worth is paper wealth, not cash; how Tesla's 2018 pay package quietly built the "Elon premium" that made everything else possible; why a rocket company is suddenly valued like an AI platform; and what the concentration of this much capital in one pair of hands means for compute, the power grid, and entry-level white-collar work.

No hype. Just the structure underneath the story — and why, if you care about AI, you now have to care about capital, infrastructure, and who owns the platforms.

⏱️ Chapters below.
0:00 The headline everyone gets wrong
1:09 What the milestone actually is
2:29 The real engine was Tesla (not the way you think)
3:50 Why SpaceX was the decisive move
4:50 The AI pivot that changed the valuation
6:27 The "impossible until it wasn't" pattern
7:28 What it actually means for AI
8:36 The ripple effects beyond AI
9:39 How it actually hits you

🔔 If you want no-hype breakdowns like this one, subscribe — it genuinely helps the channel.

💬 Is this level of concentration a feature or a bug? Tell me in the comments — there's a real argument on both sides. Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/

videos.title: Trades & Speculation | Morning Cuppa Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
Trades & Speculation | Morning Cuppa Hockey It's a stacked Wednesday on Morning Cuppa Hockey... Carter Hutton sits in for Jonny Lazarus alongside Colby Cohen for a Season 3 episode that lands right in the thick of the NHL's wildest week of the summer... and The Fourth Period's David Pagnotta joins the show off the top to make sense of all of it.

The Maple Leafs dropped the hammer on a new head coach... Jim Hiller is officially the 41st bench boss in franchise history after John Chayka and company canvassed more than two dozen candidates before circling back to a familiar face from the Mike Babcock era. Carter and Colby dig into what Hiller's hire actually means for Auston Matthews and William Nylander... and Pagnotta brings the insider lens on why Toronto zeroed in on him after a search that dragged through names like Patrick Roy and Joe Pavelski. Plus, the latest on Matthew Knies, Darnell Nurse, Ryan Craig, Dylan Larkin, and Mike Babcock. 

It's silly season in full swing, and nobody covers it better than the guy who broke half of it.

Don't miss a second of the daily hockey talk!

videos.title: Midsommarfest med Matkoma-Anton
videos.summary_source_text:
Midsommarfest med Matkoma-Anton Havets Wallenbergare:
400 g lax
400 g torsk
1 bit varmrökt lax
2 dl vispgrädde
2 äggulor
zest av en citron
1 spansk peppar
1 liten bunt dill
2 dl ströbröd
1 tsk salt
vitpeppar
olja och smör till stekningen

Kaptenens Skagenröra:
några hundra gram grovhackade räkor
1 msk löjrom
2 dl majonnäs
2 dl creme fraiche
en liten finhackad rödlök
ett litet knippe finhackad dill
salt och peppar

Smörsvängda ärtor:
400 g små fina ärtor
1 schalottenlök
några msk smör
saften från en halv pressad citron

Servera med nykokt färskpotatis

videos.title: 50% of Heart Attack Victims Had “Normal” Lab Results
videos.summary_source_text:
50% of Heart Attack Victims Had “Normal” Lab Results Is LDL really the “bad cholesterol”? Uncover the truth behind common cholesterol myths, what high LDL and cholesterol levels actually mean, and whether elevated LDL is as dangerous as you’ve been led to believe.

0:00 Is LDL bad?
0:21 LDL cholesterol explained 
2:00 Two types of LDL cholesterol
2:38 Advanced lipid profile test
3:56 Cholesterol research
6:40 Cholesterol and heart disease prevention
10:52 Clogged arteries and LDL
11:28 Large-buoyant LDL vs. small-dense LDL 
11:48 Statins 
13:07 High cholesterol levels in healthy people

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/43M2FUm

Explore the hidden factors behind heart disease, cholesterol levels, and cardiovascular health:
▶️ https://youtu.be/W9qSb6--XfE
▶️ https://youtu.be/dQqXD-8osb8
▶️ https://youtu.be/hq9Al6LR2O4

Read this article to learn more about your cholesterol levels: https://www.drberg.com/blog/how-to-read-and-understand-cholesterol-numbers

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

LDL is a protein made by the liver and serves many important functions in the body. LDL provides the raw materials needed to produce hormones such as estrogen, testosterone, and cortisol. It is also involved in the production of vitamin D and bile.

More than half of all heart attacks occur in people with normal LDL levels, raising important questions about the role of LDL in cardiovascular disease.

There are two main types of LDL: small, dense LDL and large, buoyant LDL. Small, dense LDL is associated with inflammation, insulin resistance, and chronic stress. Large, buoyant LDL is generally considered non-pathogenic. 

Despite the focus on lowering LDL cholesterol, there is limited evidence that simply reducing LDL levels acts as heart disease prevention. Higher LDL levels have even been associated with longevity in some research.

Many people are prescribed statins without determining which type of LDL they have. An advanced lipid profile can help identify whether your LDL is primarily small dense LDL or large buoyant LDL, providing a more complete picture of your cardiovascular health.

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/43M2FUm

Explore the hidden factors behind heart disease, cholesterol levels, and cardiovascular health:
▶️ https://youtu.be/W9qSb6--XfE
▶️ https://youtu.be/dQqXD-8osb8
▶️ https://youtu.be/hq9Al6LR2O4

Read this article to learn more about your cholesterol levels: https://www.drberg.com/blog/how-to-read-and-understand-cholesterol-numbers

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

LDL is a protein made by the liver and serves many important functions in the body. LDL provides the raw materials needed to produce hormones such as estrogen, testosterone, and cortisol. It is also involved in the production of vitamin D and bile.

More than half of all heart attacks occur in people with normal LDL levels, raising important questions about the role of LDL in cardiovascular disease.

There are two main types of LDL: small, dense LDL and large, buoyant LDL. Small, dense LDL is associated with inflammation, insulin resistance, and chronic stress. Large, buoyant LDL is generally considered non-pathogenic. 

Despite the focus on lowering LDL cholesterol, there is limited evidence that simply reducing LDL levels acts as heart disease prevention. Higher LDL levels have even been associated with longevity in some research.

Many people are prescribed statins without determining which type of LDL they have. An advanced lipid profile can help identify whether your LDL is primarily small dense LDL or large buoyant LDL, providing a more complete picture of your cardiovascular health.

videos.title: BMW X5M v Range Rover SV: DRAG RACE
videos.summary_source_text:
BMW X5M v Range Rover SV: DRAG RACE We’re drag racing a Range Rover SV, Range Rover OCTA & BMW X5 M over the quarter-mile and half-mile, while also comparing them in a brake test from 100mph.

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-19-05

It’s time for a 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 drag race! 

All three cars in this drag race have the exact same BMW 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 engine under their bonnets. However, that’s where the similarities end! So let’s take a closer look at each car. 

First up is the Range Rover SV, which produces 635hp and 800Nm, and it weighs in at 2,485kg. This particular car is valued at £139,995. 

On the other side of the line-up we have the OCTA. It also sends 635hp & 800Nm to all four wheels, but it’s also the heaviest car here, tipping the scales at 2,510kg! What’s more, it’s also the most expensive here, rolling up with a price tag of £158,245kg. 

Then finally we have the BMW. It claims to ‘only’ produce 625hp and 750Nm, but it’s also both the lightest and cheapest car here, coming in at 2,440kg and £137,330 respectively. 

The question is, is BMW telling the truth with the power output in the X5 M? There’s only one way to find out for sure… LET’S RACE! 

Thanks to everyone who helped us set up this race:
- OCTA: https://www.instagram.com/thecarfilmingsafetyguy/ 
- SV: https://www.instagram.com/ian911evans/

videos.title: CULTURE DEFINED! How the 2025-26 Carolina Hurricanes Embodied "Carolina Culture"
videos.summary_source_text:
CULTURE DEFINED! How the 2025-26 Carolina Hurricanes Embodied "Carolina Culture" When it comes to culture, the Carolina Hurricanes set the standard for it. That said, what is "culture defining" when it comes to Carolina? On the Thursday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on how the Hurricanes embodied what it means to be a Carolina Hurricane in 2025-26. Every season we hear about how it starts with Rod Brind'Amour and how the culture makes Raleigh special. While that is true, there seems to be a sense that this iteration took that to heart and won a cup based on that mindset. Secondly, it was revealed that the "Blue Collar Press" after workouts was getting this team ready to lift the Stanley Cup one day. How do things like that give what happened more gravitas after the fact? What is it about this team that makes things different compared not only to the NHL but sports as a whole? Finally, Zach wraps up the show by sharing his thoughts on even off the ice, the Hurricanes are set up for the future. When it comes to the roster, they're in a great place. All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: Texas Rangers Starters MacKenzie Gore and Kumar Rocker Struggle vs Twins | DLLS Rangers Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Texas Rangers Starters MacKenzie Gore and Kumar Rocker Struggle vs Twins | DLLS Rangers Podcast Become a DIEHARD! alldlls.com/diehard

An ALLCITY Network Production

Texas Rangers starting pitchers MacKenzie Gore and Kumar Rocker weren’t good enough to beat the Minnesota Twins in the first two games of their three-game series at Globe Life Field, and the offense didn’t do nearly enough (especially Tuesday). The mound woes have been part of a season-long issue for what was supposed to be the strength of the team. Jacob deGrom has been the best starter, but even Nathan Eovaldi has had his ups and downs. The Rangers are also battling injuries and have dig into their farm system. They might have to keep digging.

videos.title: 17.06.26 - RIFT Training
videos.summary_source_text:
17.06.26 - RIFT Training 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

videos.title: Hindi - 17.06.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi - 17.06.26 - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

videos.title: Zakhar Bardakov Goes Home To Russia. Stop With the Hurricanes Dynasty Talk Already.
videos.summary_source_text:
Zakhar Bardakov Goes Home To Russia. Stop With the Hurricanes Dynasty Talk Already. Colorado Avalanche lose promising forward Zakar Bardakov to the KHL as he returns home to Russia after just one NHL season. Will his departure open opportunities for other young Avalanche players, or does it highlight ongoing challenges in integrating Russian talent into the lineup?

Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson debate Bardakov’s decision, its impact on the Avalanche’s salary cap, and what it means for the team’s roster strategy moving forward. The episode also features an in-depth season grade for Nathan MacKinnon, discussions on the pitfalls of declaring hockey dynasties too soon after Carolina Hurricanes’ Stanley Cup win, and insights into how Colorado can refine their power play and offensive identity. Is it time for the Avalanche to shake up their playing style, or will minor adjustments keep them in the Stanley Cup conversation?

John E. Sokolowski-Imagn Images

videos.title: GPT-Realtime-2 Voice Agent: I Build It Live
videos.summary_source_text:
GPT-Realtime-2 Voice Agent: I Build It Live 👉 Join the community to build live and get Tank: https://mrc.fm/cmc

OpenAI quietly shipped GPT-Realtime-2, GPT-5 class reasoning with a 128k context running in real time voice, and almost nobody has built with it live yet. So I am building a voice agent with it from scratch in one sitting. Watch me wire up the Realtime API, give it a voice, hand it real tasks and debug the whole thing on camera (a half working thing on stream teaches the room more than a polished demo).

Straight after this I take it private into the members only Build Clinic, where we plug the voice agent into Tank and use it to replace OpenClaw. That is the build you voted for. Come build it with me.

videos.title: Why poor weight shift kills iron compression #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Why poor weight shift kills iron compression #golfswing #golftips Many golfers make what feels like a good swing but still struggle to compress their irons. When pressure stays on the back foot and doesn’t move into the lead side, the low point shifts too far back, leading to thin strikes and weak ball flight. Improving weight shift helps you strike the ball first and produce more powerful, consistent contact.
If iron strike or compression is holding your game back, head to the link in our bio. Our coaching plans give you clear guidance on weight shift, low point, and impact so you can hit your irons with confidence.

videos.title: China’s New AI Is 6X More Efficient Than Claude
videos.summary_source_text:
China’s New AI Is 6X More Efficient Than Claude China just dropped two new open-weight coding models, Kimi K2.7 Code and GLM-5.2, and the timing could not be more important. Kimi is built for coding agents that work through real codebases, while GLM-5.2 is pushing long-horizon software tasks with a 1 million token context window. The wild part is that both models are open, efficient, and already putting serious pressure on closed AI coding systems.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com

📌 What You’ll See:
Moonshot AI’s Kimi K2.7 Code model release
SOURCE: https://huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2.7-Code
China’s Z.ai GLM-5.2 tops GPT-5.5 on key benchmarks
SOURCE: https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/tech/artificial-intelligence/chinas-z-ai-glm-5-2-tops-openais-gpt-5-5-model-on-key-benchmarks/articleshow/131805202.cms
SpaceX to acquire Cursor developer Anysphere for $60 billion
SOURCE: https://cryptobriefing.com/spacex-acquire-cursor-developer-anysphere-60-billion-all-stock-deal/
OpenAI prepares major ChatGPT voice upgrade with GPT-Bidi-1
SOURCE: https://www.testingcatalog.com/openai-prepares-major-chatgpt-voice-upgrade-with-gpt-bidi-1/

🚨 Why It Matters
This is bigger than another coding model. Kimi K2.7 Code and GLM-5.2 show that open AI models are becoming powerful enough for real developer workflows, not just demos. If companies can run serious coding agents without depending fully on closed models, the AI race changes fast.

videos.title: Daily Faceoff NHL Free Agency Frenzy LIVE 2026: Full Coverage, Signings & Instant Reactions
videos.summary_source_text:
Daily Faceoff NHL Free Agency Frenzy LIVE 2026: Full Coverage, Signings & Instant Reactions The biggest day of the NHL offseason has arrived, and Daily Faceoff is bringing you wall-to-wall coverage of every signing, trade, rumor, and surprise move throughout the opening day of NHL Free Agency. Join Jeff Marek and Tyler Yaremchuk for a special all-day live Free Agency Frenzy on July 1st as they break down every major contract, analyze the biggest decisions around the league, and react in real time as teams reshape their rosters for the 2026-27 NHL season.

From superstar signings and blockbuster trades to under-the-radar additions that could have a major impact on Stanley Cup contenders, we'll have every angle covered throughout one of the busiest and most unpredictable days on the NHL calendar. Whether teams are making aggressive pushes to win now, retooling for the future, or navigating the salary cap to stay competitive, Daily Faceoff will be your destination for instant reactions and expert analysis.

Jeff and Tyler will be joined throughout the day by an all-star lineup of hockey insiders, analysts, and former NHL players including Jonny Lazarus, Colby Cohen, David Pagnotta, Carter Hutton, Irfaan Gaffar, and Matt Larkin, with additional special guests dropping in as the day unfolds. Expect breaking news, insider reporting, contract analysis, salary cap implications, and immediate reactions to every major move around the NHL.

As free agents come off the board and teams continue to make trades throughout the day, we'll examine which organizations improved the most, which contenders took the biggest swings, and which franchises may have altered the landscape of the Stanley Cup race. David Pagnotta will provide the latest information from around the league while our panel offers instant breakdowns of every significant signing and transaction.

We'll also track the biggest storylines heading into the day, including the top unrestricted free agents available, potential sign-and-trade scenarios, emerging contenders, and the teams under the most pressure to improve. Once the dust settles, we'll hand out winners and losers, evaluate the best contracts signed, and discuss which moves could have the biggest impact on the upcoming NHL season.

If you want the fastest reactions, the smartest analysis, and the most comprehensive coverage of NHL Free Agency, make sure you tune in live and join the conversation in the chat.

Subscribe to the Daily Faceoff YouTube channel so you never miss breaking NHL news, insider reporting, trade analysis, free agency coverage, and daily hockey content throughout the year.

Bo Nix takes another step in the Denver Broncos’ final practice of the offseason Bo Nix is back on the practice field, and the Denver Broncos’ offense is beginning to take shape around Jaylen Waddle, Sean Payton and new offensive coordinator Davis Webb.

The DNVR Broncos Crew breaks down the biggest takeaways from Denver Broncos mandatory minicamp, including the latest on Bo Nix’s recovery, his developing connection with Jaylen Waddle and what Denver’s offense could look like during the 2026 NFL season. How close is Bo Nix to being fully healthy? Can Jaylen Waddle provide the explosive element the Broncos have been missing? And what stood out from Sean Payton’s team during practice?

Plus, the guys discuss the latest Broncos news, key position battles, players who are raising their stock and what Denver must accomplish before the start of training camp.

videos.title: Stop Running GPT-5.5 Wrong — The Setup That Actually Works
videos.summary_source_text:
Stop Running GPT-5.5 Wrong — The Setup That Actually Works Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Almost everyone runs GPT-5.5 wrong on day one — they either fire one vague prompt and call it a review, or dive into code and lose the room in 90 seconds. This is the setup that actually works, and the twist is that running GPT-5.5 at its best isn't the most powerful setting. It's almost the opposite.

In this walkthrough you'll get a copy-in-minutes setup: what GPT-5.5 actually is, where it really runs (ChatGPT vs the API vs an open-weight stand-in), why restraint beats cranking every dial, what a real demo costs, and where it quietly breaks. Live demos included — ChatGPT, the Responses API, streaming, and Code Interpreter — plus an honest look at the benchmarks instead of just reading the press release.

videos.title: Bobby Marks on P-Wat, Jokic's Extension, AG Trade, What The Nuggets Should Do this Offseason & More
videos.summary_source_text:
Bobby Marks on P-Wat, Jokic's Extension, AG Trade, What The Nuggets Should Do this Offseason & More Bobby Marks joins Harrison Wind of the DNVR Nuggets Podcast to break down a massive Denver Nuggets offseason, including Peyton Watson’s restricted free agency, Nikola Jokic’s potential supermax extension, and how the second apron could shape Denver’s roster decisions. Marks explains why P-Wat could be the Nuggets’ top priority, what Jokic’s next contract might look like, and whether Aaron Gordon, Cam Johnson, or Christian Braun could be moved in a trade. With the Nuggets trying to build around Jokic after a first-round playoff exit, this conversation dives into salary cap pressure, trade options, free agency, and what Denver should do next.

videos.title: I HATED the look of these "brand killer" irons.. until I hit them
videos.summary_source_text:
I HATED the look of these "brand killer" irons.. until I hit them The McLaren Irons are embarrassingly good? I know that is a bold statement, but after putting them head to head against one of the biggest brands in golf, the results genuinely surprised me.

Subscribe to this channel @AlexElliottReviews 

If you are looking for the best irons of 2026, want to hit your irons longer, or are searching for a genuine big brand killer, this is a test you need to see.

In this video, I compare the McLaren Irons against one of the most popular iron sets in golf, looking at distance, forgiveness, consistency, feel, ball speed, and overall performance.

We cover McLaren Irons review, best irons of 2026, how to hit irons longer, longest golf irons tested, forgiving irons for mid handicap golfers, big brand killer golf clubs, distance iron comparison, golf iron technology explained, iron fitting advice, players distance irons, game improvement irons, and whether McLaren Irons can compete with the biggest names in golf.

The results were not what I expected. In some areas, the McLaren Irons delivered performance that could genuinely challenge the biggest brands in the game.

Could these be the most underrated irons of 2026?

Watch until the end to see the full data and let me know in the comments whether you would put the McLaren Irons in your bag.

videos.title: WAKE UP WITH A DRY MOUTH?  DO THIS TONIGHT  |  Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
WAKE UP WITH A DRY MOUTH?  DO THIS TONIGHT  |  Dr. Mandell Do you wake up every morning with a dry mouth, dry throat, cracked lips, bad breath, or an overwhelming need to drink water before you can even speak? While many people assume dehydration is the cause, the real reason may be something entirely different. In this video, Dr. Alan Mandell explains the common causes of waking up with a dry mouth, including mouth breathing, nasal congestion, allergies, snoring, sleep apnea, medications, elevated blood sugar, stress, alcohol, caffeine, and other hidden factors that can affect saliva production during sleep. You'll learn why saliva is essential for protecting your teeth, gums, digestion, and overall health, and discover simple steps you can take tonight to improve nasal breathing, support healthy saliva production, and wake up feeling more refreshed tomorrow morning. Sometimes the smallest symptoms can reveal important clues about your health. Watch until the end and learn what your body may be trying to tell you while you sleep. 


Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

videos.summary_source_text:
Two Secrets To Unlock Your Shot If your shot feels locked up, it very well could be your strength or your stick. But it could also be the advice you were given and how you were taught that is limiting your power.

The uncomfortable part is shooting like the top 1% means doing the exact things a lot of today’s shooting coaches tell you never to do. The two cues you’ve heard your whole life, keep your hands out away from your body and just transfer your weight forward, could be the same two cues capping your power.

The best shooters in the world do the opposite where they pull the top hand back in toward the body and they drop the back hip to load the posterior oblique sling.

That’s the gap between the NHL’s best and everyone else. It usually isn’t talent or a stiffer flex although these play a role they are secondary after your shooting mechanics.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: What This Cardiologist Learned from Thousands of Patients
videos.summary_source_text:
What This Cardiologist Learned from Thousands of Patients As a cardiologist I spend every day looking at the consequences of disease. Blocked arteries. Heart attacks. Stents. Procedures. But after seeing the same patterns over and over, my attention shifted upstream. The patients with the worst outcomes often shared a common characteristic: high amounts of visceral fat. That observation led to a bigger question. Could reducing visceral fat be one of the most effective forms of prevention?

This is a clip from my conversation on The Primal Podcast. You can find the full convesation on their channel or in the collaborations on my channel.

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

Subscribe to the Channel! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOtQHehGWtblMp1gZC8Kq3Q?sub_confirmation=1

Remember that my videos are not a substitute for individualized medical care by a qualified physician. Always consult your physicians before making any diet, nutrition, medication, or lifestyle changes.


About Dr. Pradip Jamnadas, MD, MBBS, FACC, FSCAI, FCCP, FACP

The founder and Chief Medical Officer of Cardiovascular Interventions, P.A. in Orlando Florida where, since 1990,  he has been repeatedly recognized in local publications as a Top Doctor performing thousands of interventional procedures in hospital and out patient settings. As a consultant cardiologist with a large diversified inpatient and outpatient practice he is noted for his passions for teaching and illuminating prevention for cardiovascular disease . videos.title: Leafs Hire Jim Hiller & Eric Tulsky Joins the Show ft. Brian Burke | The Sheet with Jeff Marek
videos.summary_source_text:
Leafs Hire Jim Hiller & Eric Tulsky Joins the Show ft. Brian Burke | The Sheet with Jeff Marek The NHL offseason is officially underway, and the fireworks have already begun. On today's edition of The Sheet, Jeff Marek is joined by Brian Burke to break down what could be one of the wildest summers the league has seen in years. From blockbuster trade speculation to front office decisions that could reshape contenders, the duo dives into the biggest storylines around the NHL. They discuss the Toronto Maple Leafs making headlines with the surprising trade that sent Joseph Woll and Simon Benoit to the Philadelphia Flyers, what the return means for Toronto, and how the move impacts both organizations moving forward. They also react to the Leafs' unexpected decision to hire Jim Hiller as the franchise's new head coach and examine what his coaching style could bring to a team under immense pressure to win.

Later, Carolina Hurricanes General Manager Eric Tulsky joins the show fresh off a Stanley Cup championship season. Tulsky reflects on building a championship roster in Carolina, the organization's long-term vision, and the key decisions that helped bring the Stanley Cup back to Raleigh. He discusses the acquisition of Logan Stankoven, balancing analytics with traditional hockey evaluation, managing a modern NHL front office, and how his unique path to becoming a Stanley Cup-winning GM has shaped his approach to team building. Plus, insight into the Hurricanes' championship run, lessons learned along the way, and what's next for one of the NHL's model franchises.

📺 Subscribe for more NHL news, trade rumours, Stanley Cup coverage, Maple Leafs analysis, and exclusive interviews with the biggest names in hockey.

videos.title: DeepMind's New Paper: How AI Goes From Human-Level to Superintelligent
videos.summary_source_text:
DeepMind's New Paper: How AI Goes From Human-Level to Superintelligent Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everyone treats AGI like the final boss — human-level AI shows up, the story ends. But Google DeepMind's newest paper, "From AGI to ASI" (published June 10, 2026), argues the opposite: AGI isn't the finish line. It might just be the starting gun.

In this video I break down what one of the world's top AI labs is now saying happens AFTER human-level AI — the four pathways from AGI to superintelligence, who thinks it's coming and when, and why the smartest people in the room still can't agree. No hype, no doom. Just the actual argument.

We get the vocabulary straight (narrow AI → AGI → ASI), unpack what DeepMind is officially on record saying, look at the projects pointing the way (Genie 3, Gemini Robotics, SIMA 2, Co-Scientist, AlphaEvolve), and weigh the believers against the skeptics — from METR's task-length doubling to Stanford's "jagged" frontier systems. The real stakes turn out to be about power and control, not killer robots.

Where do you land? Drop your AGI year in the comments — 2027? 2030? 2047? — and let's see if the evidence moves you.

08:28 Why the Skeptics Push Back
09:36 The Real Stakes: Power, Not Robots

If you want the actual argument — not the hype and not the doom — subscribe. This channel lives in that gray area on purpose.

#agi  #deepmind  #superintelligence  #ainews  #ASI Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everyone treats AGI like the final boss — human-level AI shows up, the story ends. But Google DeepMind's newest paper, "From AGI to ASI" (published June 10, 2026), argues the opposite: AGI isn't the finish line. It might just be the starting gun.

In this video I break down what one of the world's top AI labs is now saying happens AFTER human-level AI — the four pathways from AGI to superintelligence, who thinks it's coming and when, and why the smartest people in the room still can't agree. No hype, no doom. Just the actual argument.

We get the vocabulary straight (narrow AI → AGI → ASI), unpack what DeepMind is officially on record saying, look at the projects pointing the way (Genie 3, Gemini Robotics, SIMA 2, Co-Scientist, AlphaEvolve), and weigh the believers against the skeptics — from METR's task-length doubling to Stanford's "jagged" frontier systems. The real stakes turn out to be about power and control, not killer robots.

Where do you land? Drop your AGI year in the comments — 2027? 2030? 2047? — and let's see if the evidence moves you.

videos.title: Should the Mavs Draft Mikel Brown Jr? | Slightly Biased Makes the Case
videos.summary_source_text:
Should the Mavs Draft Mikel Brown Jr? | Slightly Biased Makes the Case Could the Dallas Mavericks land one of the biggest steals in the NBA Draft?

Kevin Gray welcomes Slightly Biased to the DLLS Mavs Podcast to break down Louisville guard Mikel Brown Jr. and whether he's the right pick for the Mavericks if he's still on the board at No. 9. The guys discuss Brown's fit alongside Cooper Flagg and Anthony Davis, his upside as a lead guard, and whether Dallas should prioritize playmaking in the draft.

They also dive into Michigan big man Aday Mara, one of the most intriguing prospects in the class. Can Mara become a long-term NBA starter? Would he make sense for the Mavericks as a developmental big?

Subscribe to DLLS Sports for daily Mavericks coverage, breaking news, live shows, and NBA Draft analysis.

videos.title: Sir Nick Faldo Looks Back on His First Major Victory | The Rewind
videos.summary_source_text:
Sir Nick Faldo Looks Back on His First Major Victory | The Rewind SUBSCRIBE -  https://bit.ly/RandaGolfSubscribe

Sir Nick Faldo revisits one of the most important weeks of his career as he looks back on his victory at the 1987 Open Championship at Muirfield.

In this episode of The Rewind, Faldo watches back the defining moments from the championship that delivered his first major title. Following years of rebuilding his swing, the Englishman arrived at Muirfield ready to contend and produced one of the most memorable final rounds in Open history, famously carding 18 consecutive pars to lift the Claret Jug.

videos.title: Trade Rumors Heating Up + Jim Hiller Takes Over in Toronto | DFO Live
videos.summary_source_text:
Trade Rumors Heating Up + Jim Hiller Takes Over in Toronto | DFO Live The NHL offseason is officially heating up.

Tyler Yaremchuk and Carter Hutton react to the Colorado Avalanche trading Ross Colton to the Nashville Predators and discuss what it could mean for the rest of Colorado's summer plans. Is this just the beginning of a busy offseason for the Avalanche?

The guys also dive into the latest reporting from David Pagnotta surrounding Morgan Rielly's future in Toronto. Will Rielly still be a Maple Leaf next season, or could the organization look to make a major change after hiring Jim Hiller as the franchise's new head coach?

Another hot topic is the reported possibility of Dan Vladar landing a massive contract in Philadelphia. Would a five-year, $25 million deal make sense for the Flyers, or would it be another example of a team overpaying for goaltending in free agency?

The offseason preview series continues with a look at the Boston Bruins. What should be priority number one for Don Sweeney this summer? What would a dream offseason look like as Boston tries to get back into Stanley Cup contention?

Brent Wallace joins the show for The All 32 to discuss the latest on Linus Ullmark, Brady Tkachuk, Jordan Spence, and other names generating buzz around the NHL. @Cominginhotsens

videos.title: PewDiePie’s FREE Odysseus AI (Full Review & Setup)
videos.summary_source_text:
PewDiePie’s FREE Odysseus AI (Full Review & Setup) Try Recraft V4.1 now and experience an image generation model with exceptional visual taste : https://go.recraft.ai/MattWolfe

PewDiePie built an open source AI workspace called Project Odysseus and I put it through its paces so you don't have to.

Odysseus is a self-hosted AI platform that lets you run local AI models, connect APIs, do deep research, manage files, and keep your data completely off the cloud. Think ChatGPT or Claude but running on your own machine, with your own models, and full privacy control.

In this video I install it on my M3 Mac, connect local models through Ollama, run model comparisons between Gemma 3 12B and GPT 5.5, test the deep research module, and explore every feature the app has to offer including the brain memory system, the model comparison scoreboard, and the image editor (spoiler: some things work great, some things I absolutely could not get to work no matter how hard I tried).

If you've ever wanted to run AI completely offline without paying API fees or sending your data to a server, this is worth knowing about.

videos.title: Who will win Closest to the Pin @ the Floating Umbrella?! | Travelers Championship 15 1/2 Challenge
videos.summary_source_text:
Who will win Closest to the Pin @ the Floating Umbrella?! | Travelers Championship 15 1/2 Challenge Tune in to see top golf creators Roger Steele, Josh Kelley, Tisha Alyn, alongside New England legend Julian Edelman, compete in a LIVE closest to the pin challenge at the Travelers Championship’s iconic Umbrella at 15 1/2, hosted by Carson Daly. With help from Scottie Scheffler, Tommy Fleetwood, Justin Thomas, and Keegan Bradley, each Creator will pair with one of the PGA TOUR’s best, aiming to achieve the most points possible in 15 ½ minutes.  One point is earned for each ball that stays on the Umbrella at 15 ½ when it comes to rest; and ten points are awarded for every hole-in-one!  The winning team will earn a donation to their charity of choice, all thanks to Travelers!

videos.title: Could These Golf Clubs Change Golf FOREVER?
videos.summary_source_text:
Could These Golf Clubs Change Golf FOREVER? Same-length irons have always been one of golf's most debated concepts. But what happens when you combine the consistency of single-length irons with the distance benefits of a traditional set?

Rick puts the new Avoda Combo Length irons to the test to find out if this unique design can genuinely help golfers hit better shots and lower scores.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

This can feel responsible — but it may also keep your nervous system in a state of vigilance.
In this video, I explore the difference between awareness and self-monitoring, and why the quality of attention you bring to your body matters so much.

This idea is also at the heart of my book, Your Body Is Not Broken, where I explore a gentler way to understand pain, tension, fear of movement, and the habit of trying to fix yourself: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0GX363HMX

videos.title: Knicks Win the NBA Finals, Avalanche Offseason Questions & Remembering Doug Moe | After the Horn
videos.summary_source_text:
Knicks Win the NBA Finals, Avalanche Offseason Questions & Remembering Doug Moe | After the Horn The Larry O'Brien Trophy has been handed out, the Stanley Cup has a new home, and Denver sports fans have plenty to talk about.

On this week's episode of After the Horn, Woody Paige, Tom Green, and Jeff Morton break down the New York Knicks' NBA championship run and debate whether this team deserves the "greatest ever" conversation. The crew also dives into the latest Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors, explores what moves could put the Denver Nuggets back into title contention, and discusses the Colorado Avalanche's offseason outlook following another disappointing playoff exit.

The conversation shifts to the Colorado Rockies, their looming trade deadline decisions, and whether meaningful change could finally be on the horizon.

Plus, the panel reflects on one of the most emotional moments in recent Denver sports history: the celebration of life honoring Nuggets legend Doug Moe. Friends, former players, media members, and family gathered to share stories and remember the coach whose impact extended far beyond basketball.

videos.title: Memory Loss Has Nothing to Do with Your Age (Watch Out for This Instead)
videos.summary_source_text:
Memory Loss Has Nothing to Do with Your Age (Watch Out for This Instead) Memory loss and cognitive decline are not just a normal part of aging. Discover why dementia is often called type 3 diabetes, how insulin resistance affects brain function, and what you can do to improve memory, support cognitive function, and combat cognitive decline naturally.

0:00 Aging and memory loss
0:51 The stages of cognitive decline
1:30 Memory loss begins 
2:15 PET scan to assess memory problems
2:57 Type 3 diabetes and insulin resistance explained
4:04 Are your memory problems related to blood sugar control?
4:55 High blood sugar, insulin resistance, and vitamin B1
7:49 Alzheimer’s prevention 
8:15 How much benfotiamine for brain health

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4a6BEP1

Explore more information on dementia prevention, early symptoms, and cognitive decline:
▶️ https://youtu.be/OErFtjHismU
▶️ https://youtu.be/n8zzRq8aMo8
▶️ https://youtu.be/XHNOAvhkr5Q

Discover the benefits of Benfotiamine here: 
▶️ https://youtu.be/83sOzga-i0I

Looking for ways to improve memory and mental clarity? Read this article: https://drbrg.co/44d98Yu

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Symptoms of cognitive decline can begin long before a person develops dementia or Alzheimer’s disease.

In the early stages, you may experience a loss of brain energy, a buildup of protein in the brain, and shrinkage of the hippocampus. At this point, most people are not experiencing obvious memory loss. By stage 3, you may start to notice difficulty remembering names, decreased learning ability, and other subtle signs of cognitive decline. These early symptoms can begin as early as your 30s and 40s.

Alzheimer’s disease is often described as type 3 diabetes because it involves insulin resistance in the brain. Even though plenty of glucose is available, your neurons can no longer access and use that fuel efficiently.

If you need to eat frequently to avoid irritability, fatigue, or brain fog, it may be an early sign of blood sugar problems and insulin resistance.

High blood sugar and chronically elevated insulin can deplete vitamin B1, reducing the brain’s ability to produce energy. A diet high in sugar and refined carbohydrates can increase the risk of vitamin B1 deficiency.

One of the most important things you can do for dementia prevention is to reduce your sugar and carbohydrate intake. Benfotiamine can help support brain health, but it will not be nearly as effective if you continue consuming a high-carb diet.

Memory loss and cognitive decline are not just a normal part of aging. Discover why dementia is often called type 3 diabetes, how insulin resistance affects brain function, and what you can do to improve memory, support cognitive function, and combat cognitive decline naturally.

Symptoms of cognitive decline can begin long before a person develops dementia or Alzheimer’s disease.

In the early stages, you may experience a loss of brain energy, a buildup of protein in the brain, and shrinkage of the hippocampus. At this point, most people are not experiencing obvious memory loss. By stage 3, you may start to notice difficulty remembering names, decreased learning ability, and other subtle signs of cognitive decline. These early symptoms can begin as early as your 30s and 40s.

Alzheimer’s disease is often described as type 3 diabetes because it involves insulin resistance in the brain. Even though plenty of glucose is available, your neurons can no longer access and use that fuel efficiently.

If you need to eat frequently to avoid irritability, fatigue, or brain fog, it may be an early sign of blood sugar problems and insulin resistance.

High blood sugar and chronically elevated insulin can deplete vitamin B1, reducing the brain’s ability to produce energy. A diet high in sugar and refined carbohydrates can increase the risk of vitamin B1 deficiency.

One of the most important things you can do for dementia prevention is to reduce your sugar and carbohydrate intake. Benfotiamine can help support brain health, but it will not be nearly as effective if you continue consuming a high-carb diet.

videos.title: Tom Kristensen: The Best Who Never Raced In F1? | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Tom Kristensen: The Best Who Never Raced In F1? | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast As a child, Formula 1 was his dream. As a young driver, he was on the same trajectory as Michael Schumacher. Tom Kristensen was offered a chance to join a Formula 1 team, but he saw it as too risky. Instead, he found success in sportscars and made history at the Le Mans 24 Hours with a record nine victories.

Tom tells Tom Clarkson about the ‘risky’ offer he received from a Formula 1 team in the 1990s, the sportscar decision which harmed his F1 chances, and the day he impressed a World Champion in a modern F1 car.

‘Mr Le Mans’ also remembers his history-making days at the world’s most famous endurance race and explains how racing different cars made him a better driver.

TOUR EXCLUSIVES in the Titleist tour van 🤯 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.
